name: Athletics
description: Run, jump, and move with speed or grace; covers sprinting, leaping gaps, and navigating difficult terrain at pace.

name: Bribery
description: Judge how likely a target is to accept a bribe and negotiate the appropriate amount to offer without causing offence or suspicion.

name: Charm
description: Make people think favourably of you, win friends, and influence behaviour through pleasant manner, flattery, or personal magnetism.

name: Charm Animal
description: Befriend, calm, or subjugate animals through patient communication and an understanding of animal behaviour.

name: Climb
description: Ascend steep or vertical surfaces such as walls, cliffs, and rigging, finding handholds and hauling yourself upward.

name: Cool
description: Remain calm under stress, suppress panic, and resist Fear and Terror effects through sheer psychological resilience.

name: Consume Alcohol
description: Handle alcohol without clouding your judgment; drink rivals under the table while keeping your wits about you.

name: Dodge
description: Avoid attacks and hazards by moving clear at the last moment; the primary defensive response action in melee and ranged combat.

name: Drive
description: Guide carts, carriages, and other wheeled vehicles along roads and tracks with minimal mishap, including tricky manoeuvres.

name: Endurance
description: Endure hardship, deprivation, and harsh environments; also used when recovering from wounds and resisting the effects of fatigue.

name: Gamble
description: Measure the likelihood of bets paying off and excel at games of chance, knowing when to hold and when to fold.

name: Gossip
description: Ferret out local news, secrets, and rumours by chatting up the right people, or spread misinformation convincingly.

name: Haggle
description: Secure better deals when buying or selling goods by negotiating prices and terms through confident bargaining.

name: Intimidate
description: Coerce or frighten sentient creatures into compliance through displays of physical menace, threats, or sheer presence.

name: Intuition
description: Get a feel for your surroundings and notice when something is wrong; sense ambushes, deceptions, and hidden danger.

name: Leadership
description: Lead others, command their respect, and inspire them to follow your orders under pressure or in the heat of battle.

name: Navigation
description: Find your way in the wilderness or unfamiliar terrain using landmarks, the stars, maps, or dead reckoning.

name: Outdoor Survival
description: Survive in the wild by fishing, hunting, foraging for food, building shelters, and reading the weather.

name: Perception
description: Notice things with all your senses — spot hidden enemies, detect concealed objects, and pick up on subtle environmental cues.

name: Row
description: Pull an oar effectively to move a boat through water, whether crossing a calm river or fighting against the current.

name: Art (Cartography)
description: Create accurate and readable maps of regions, dungeons, and cities, capturing geography and notable features on parchment.

name: Art (Engraving)
description: Carve detailed patterns, images, and inscriptions into metal, wood, stone, or bone to create decorative or commemorative works.

name: Art (Mosaics)
description: Assemble small pieces of tile, glass, or stone into intricate images and decorative patterns for walls and floors.

name: Art (Painting)
description: Create works in paint — portraits, landscapes, religious icons, and illuminated manuscripts of aesthetic and commercial value.

name: Art (Sculpture)
description: Shape stone, clay, wood, or metal into three-dimensional figures and decorative forms ranging from small carvings to grand statues.

name: Art (Tattoo)
description: Apply permanent ink designs to skin with precision, from simple tribal marks to elaborate pictorial works of art.

name: Art (Weaving)
description: Weave threads into cloth, tapestries, and patterned textiles, combining craft skill with artistic composition.

name: Entertain (Acting)
description: Portray characters convincingly on stage or in everyday deception, controlling voice, posture, and expression to persuade an audience.

name: Entertain (Comedy)
description: Delight crowds with jests, wordplay, physical humour, and comic timing to earn laughs and goodwill.

name: Entertain (Singing)
description: Perform vocal music to entertain audiences in taverns, courts, or temples — from rousing drinking songs to devotional hymns.

name: Entertain (Storytelling)
description: Captivate listeners with spoken tales, legends, and yarns, weaving narrative tension and memorable characters through voice and pacing.

name: Melee (Basic)
description: Use common hand-to-hand weapons — swords, axes, maces, and clubs — the default melee specialisation available to nearly every martial career.

name: Melee (Brawling)
description: Fight with fists, feet, and improvised street-brawling techniques; also covers knuckledusters and other brawling weapons.

name: Melee (Cavalry)
description: Wield weapons designed for use on horseback — lances, cavalry sabres, and other arms optimised for mounted combat.

name: Melee (Fencing)
description: Use light duelling blades with trained, precise technique — rapiers and estocs favoured by duelists and Estalian swordsmen.

name: Melee (Flail)
description: Wield flails and chain weapons whose flexible construction makes them harder to parry but demands greater skill to control.

name: Melee (Parry)
description: Use weapons with the Defensive quality — bucklers, main-gauche, and parrying daggers — to deflect incoming blows.

name: Melee (Pole-Arm)
description: Wield polearms such as halberds, spears, and pikes — weapons that provide reach but require space and training to use effectively.

name: Melee (Two-Handed)
description: Swing great swords, great axes, and other two-handed weapons that trade speed for devastating damage potential.

name: Ride (Demigryph)
description: Ride and control a Demigryph — the fearsome half-eagle, half-lion beasts used as mounts by the elite Knights of the Empire.

name: Ride (Great Wolf)
description: Ride a Great Wolf, the massive predatory canines favoured as mounts by certain Chaos marauders and northern warriors.

name: Ride (Griffon)
description: Ride a Griffon, the proud eagle-lion creatures used as mounts by only the most exalted warrior princes and generals.

name: Ride (Horse)
description: Ride horses competently — controlling the mount in combat, on difficult terrain, and at full gallop without losing your seat.

name: Ride (Pegasus)
description: Ride a Pegasus, the winged horse of legend, mastering the challenges of aerial combat and airborne manoeuvring.

name: Stealth (Rural)
description: Move quietly and conceal yourself in natural environments — forests, fields, and open countryside — without being seen or heard.

name: Stealth (Underground)
description: Creep through caves, tunnels, sewers, and dungeon corridors without drawing attention, exploiting darkness and confined spaces.

name: Stealth (Urban)
description: Move unseen and unheard through streets, alleys, and buildings, slipping through crowds and hugging shadows in city environments.

name: Animal Care
description: Tend and care for animals in your charge — feed, groom, and stable them — and diagnose and treat sickness or injury.

name: Evaluate
description: Determine the true value of rare artefacts, unusual goods, gems, and works of art, spotting fakes and knowing what the market will bear.

name: Heal
description: Treat injuries, staunch bleeding, diagnose illness, and tend wounds using medical knowledge and whatever supplies are at hand.

name: Pick Lock
description: Open locks without their proper keys using picks and tension wrenches, defeating mechanical security through feel and technique.

name: Pray
description: Invoke or commune with a deity through ritual prayer to seek divine intervention, guidance, or the granting of Miracles.

name: Research
description: Pull useful information from libraries, archives, and information storehouses by knowing where to look and how to interpret what you find.

name: Set Trap
description: Set, conceal, and disarm traps of all varieties — from simple snares and pitfalls to complex mechanical devices.

name: Sleight of Hand
description: Pick pockets, palm objects, perform prestidigitation, and cheat at cards through misdirection and nimble finger work.

name: Swim
description: Swim in water without drowning — keeping afloat and making progress against currents, in armour, or while exhausted.

name: Track
description: Follow subtle trails left by others across wilderness terrain — reading footprints, broken vegetation, and other signs of passage.

name: Animal Training (Demigryph)
description: Train Demigryphs to accept riders and obey commands in battle — a rare and dangerous discipline mastered only by dedicated handlers.

name: Animal Training (Dog)
description: Train dogs to track, guard, attack on command, or perform other useful tasks through patient conditioning and reward.

name: Animal Training (Horse)
description: Train horses to perform beyond basic riding — war training, dressage, trick performance, or reliable behaviour under fire.

name: Animal Training (Pegasus)
description: Train Pegasi to accept a rider, obey commands in the air, and overcome their wild instincts — an extraordinarily difficult undertaking.

name: Animal Training (Pigeon)
description: Train carrier pigeons to return home reliably, enabling the sending of messages across long distances with reasonable speed.

name: Channelling (Aqshy)
description: Call upon and control Aqshy, the Wind of Fire — the passionate, destructive magical wind harnessed by the Bright Order.

name: Channelling (Azyr)
description: Call upon and control Azyr, the Wind of Heavens — the cold, distant magical wind of celestial prophecy mastered by the Celestial Order.

name: Channelling (Chamon)
description: Call upon and control Chamon, the Wind of Metal — the dense, alchemical wind of transformation harnessed by the Gold Order.

name: Channelling (Dhar)
description: Call upon and control Dhar, the Dark Wind — corrupted magic drawn from the blending of all Winds; the province of Chaos sorcerers and Dark Wizards.

name: Channelling (Ghur)
description: Call upon and control Ghur, the Wind of Beasts — the primal, savage magical wind that connects magic to the natural world, used by the Amber Order.

name: Channelling (Ghyran)
description: Call upon and control Ghyran, the Wind of Life — the nurturing, restorative magical wind of growth and healing harnessed by the Jade Order.

name: Channelling (Hysh)
description: Call upon and control Hysh, the Wind of Light — the pure, luminous magical wind of reason and illumination mastered by the Light Order.

name: Channelling (Shyish)
description: Call upon and control Shyish, the Wind of Death — the cold, entropic magical wind of endings and the underworld, harnessed by the Amethyst Order.

name: Channelling (Ulgu)
description: Call upon and control Ulgu, the Wind of Shadows — the grey, deceptive magical wind of illusion and misdirection mastered by the Grey Order.

name: Language (Albion)
description: Speak and understand the tongue of Albion, the mist-shrouded isle across the Great Ocean — rare knowledge in the Old World.

name: Language (Battle Tongue)
description: Use the simplified command language employed by armies to communicate orders clearly through the noise and chaos of battle.

name: Language (Bretonnian)
description: Speak and understand Bretonnian, the language of the noble kingdom to the Empire's west, prized at court and in diplomacy.

name: Language (Classical)
description: Read and write Classical, the dead scholarly language used in academic texts, religious ceremonies, and arcane notation across the Old World.

name: Language (Elthárin)
description: Speak and understand Elthárin, the melodic tongue of the High Elves and Wood Elves — complex, ancient, and rarely taught to humans.

name: Language (Estalian)
description: Speak and understand Estalian, the language of Estalia on the southern peninsula — useful for trade, travel, and dealings with Estalian merchants.

name: Language (Gospodarinyi)
description: Speak and understand Gospodarinyi, the tongue of Kislev — a harsh, guttural language invaluable for dealings in the frozen north.

name: Language (Grumbarth)
description: Speak and understand Grumbarth, the secret tongue used by Ogres — a blunt, grunted language of few words and great directness.

name: Language (Guilder)
description: Speak and understand the coded trade-cant used by guild members to communicate commercial information and signal membership.

name: Language (Khazalid)
description: Speak and understand Khazalid, the guttural tongue of the Dwarfs — rarely taught to outsiders and a mark of deep trust when it is.

name: Language (Magick)
description: Read, write, and speak the arcane tongue used to shape and direct magical energies; required to cast spells and interpret magical texts.

name: Language (Mootish)
description: Speak and understand Mootish, the quirky dialect of the Halflings of the Moot — full of food metaphors and comfortable understatement.

name: Language (Norse)
description: Speak and understand Norse, the rough tongue of the Norse raiders from the far north — useful for trade or survival in Norsca.

name: Language (Queekish)
description: Speak and understand Queekish, the chittering language of the Skaven — disturbing knowledge that hints at dangerous familiarity with the ratmen.

name: Language (Reikspiel)
description: Speak and understand Reikspiel, the common tongue of the Empire — the lingua franca of trade and politics across most of the Old World.

name: Language (Thieves' Tongue)
description: Speak and understand the secret argot of thieves and criminals — a coded patois that lets underworld denizens communicate safely in plain sight.

name: Language (Tilean)
description: Speak and understand Tilean, the language of the city-states of Tilea — the tongue of merchants, mercenaries, and Renaissance culture.

name: Language (Wastelander)
description: Speak and understand Wastelander, the dialect of the Wasteland north of Bretonnia — a Dutch-influenced tongue of seafarers and merchants.

name: Lore (Engineering)
description: Apply formal knowledge of engineering principles — construction, structural mechanics, siege equipment, and the workings of machines.

name: Lore (Geology)
description: Identify minerals, rock formations, and geological features; invaluable for miners, prospectors, and those who delve deep underground.

name: Lore (Heraldry)
description: Identify coats of arms, noble houses, and heraldic devices, understanding the social and political implications they carry.

name: Lore (History)
description: Recall and interpret the history of the Old World — past wars, dynasties, disasters, and the events that shaped the present age.

name: Lore (Law)
description: Understand Imperial law, local ordinances, and legal procedure — invaluable for lawyers, merchants, and those who wish to avoid the gallows.

name: Lore (Magick)
description: Understand the theoretical underpinnings of magic — the Winds, the Orders, and the dangers of the arcane arts, beyond the ability to cast.

name: Lore (Metallurgy)
description: Know the properties, working techniques, and identification of metals and alloys — essential for smiths, alchemists, and engineers.

name: Lore (Science)
description: Apply natural philosophy and empirical reasoning — physics, chemistry, biology, and the scientific method in an age of emerging inquiry.

name: Lore (Theology)
description: Know the doctrines, histories, and rituals of the Old World's religions — the cults of Sigmar, Ulric, Morr, and dozens of other deities.

name: Perform (Acrobatics)
description: Execute tumbles, flips, handstands, and gymnastic feats for performance or practical use in combat and infiltration.

name: Perform (Clowning)
description: Entertain audiences with physical comedy, pratfalls, and exaggerated buffoonery — a staple of travelling fairs and noble entertainments.

name: Perform (Dancing)
description: Dance with skill and grace for audiences or at social functions, from folk reels in village taverns to formal court dances.

name: Perform (Firebreathing)
description: Spectacularly breathe or manipulate fire for entertainment — a dramatic and dangerous crowd-pleasing act requiring precise technique.

name: Perform (Juggling)
description: Keep multiple objects airborne simultaneously, entertaining crowds and demonstrating exceptional hand-eye coordination.

name: Perform (Miming)
description: Convey stories, emotions, and characters through silent body movement and gesture alone, without words or props.

name: Perform (Rope Walking)
description: Walk, balance, and perform on taut ropes or narrow surfaces at height — a crowd-pleasing act with obvious practical infiltration applications.

name: Play (Bagpipe)
description: Play the bagpipe competently — a demanding instrument of drones and chanter requiring considerable lung capacity and coordination.

name: Play (Harpsichord)
description: Play the harpsichord with skill — a keyboard instrument of fine drawing rooms, chapels, and the courts of the wealthy.

name: Play (Horn)
description: Play the horn or bugle — both as a musical instrument in performance and as a signalling device on the battlefield.

name: Play (Lute)
description: Play the lute with skill — the quintessential instrument of bards, troubadours, and entertainers across the Old World.

name: Play (Violin)
description: Play the violin with skill — a versatile bowed instrument heard in village dances and aristocratic concert halls alike.

name: Ranged (Blackpowder)
description: Use blackpowder firearms — pistols, handguns, and blunderbusses — with the training needed to load, aim, and fire reliably.

name: Ranged (Bow)
description: Use shortbows and longbows with trained accuracy — the classic ranged weapon of hunters, soldiers, and Wood Elf warriors.

name: Ranged (Crossbow)
description: Use crossbows with proficiency — mechanically aimed weapons favoured by city guards and mercenaries for reliable stopping power.

name: Ranged (Engineering)
description: Operate complex ranged engineering devices — repeating handguns, steam-powered guns, and other Dwarf or Imperial innovations.

name: Ranged (Entangling)
description: Use entangling ranged weapons such as bolas and nets to restrain and immobilise targets rather than wound them.

name: Ranged (Explosives)
description: Throw or launch explosive devices — grenades, bombs, and similar weapons — with enough accuracy to hit the intended target.

name: Ranged (Sling)
description: Hurl stones and lead shot with a sling at speed and accuracy — a cheap but effective ranged weapon in trained hands.

name: Ranged (Throwing)
description: Throw weapons such as javelins, throwing axes, and knives with trained precision beyond a simple untrained hurl.

name: Sail (Barge)
description: Operate and manoeuvre a barge on rivers and canals, managing the sail, tiller, and poles to navigate slow inland waterways.

name: Sail (Caravel)
description: Operate a caravel — a light, manoeuvrable ocean-going vessel suited for exploration and trade on the open sea.

name: Sail (Cog)
description: Operate a cog — a sturdy single-masted trading vessel common on the rivers and coastal waters of the Old World.

name: Sail (Frigate)
description: Operate a frigate — a fast, well-armed warship capable of deep-ocean operations, requiring a full crew and expert seamanship.

name: Sail (Wolfship)
description: Operate a Wolfship — the deadly longship of the Norse raiders, designed for speed, coastal assault, and river penetration.

name: Secret Signs (Grey Order)
description: Recognise and use the clandestine markings of the Grey Order of Wizards, the shadowy society of Ulgu practitioners.

name: Secret Signs (Guilder)
description: Recognise and use the secret markings of the merchant guilds — symbols that communicate guild membership, prices, and warnings.

name: Secret Signs (Ranger)
description: Recognise and use the trail signs left by Rangers — carvings and natural markers that communicate direction, danger, and resources.

name: Secret Signs (Scout)
description: Recognise and use military scout markings — coded signs that relay tactical information about enemy positions and safe routes.

name: Secret Signs (Thief)
description: Recognise and use the chalk marks and scratched symbols of the thieves' underworld — communicating opportunity, danger, and territory.

name: Secret Signs (Vagabond)
description: Recognise and use the marks left by vagabonds and travellers — signs indicating safe houses, hostile locals, and generous households.

name: Trade (Apothecary)
description: Compound medicines, poultices, and potions from herbs and other ingredients, providing medicinal services to paying customers.

name: Trade (Calligrapher)
description: Produce elegant, legible script for documents, manuscripts, and official records — a valued service in a world of expensive literacy.

name: Trade (Carpenter)
description: Work wood into furniture, structural timbers, barrels, and other objects — a foundational trade of Old World towns and cities.

name: Trade (Chandler)
description: Make and sell candles, soap, and rendered products — providing essential illumination and hygiene goods to communities.

name: Trade (Cook)
description: Prepare food professionally — from basic camp fare that keeps a party going to elaborate banquet dishes for wealthy patrons.

name: Trade (Embalmer)
description: Preserve corpses against decay for burial preparation — a respected trade in service of Morr's cult and the families of the dead.

name: Trade (Smith)
description: Work metal at the forge — shaping iron and steel into tools, horseshoes, weapons, and armour for paying customers.

name: Trade (Tanner)
description: Cure and tan animal hides into leather for armour, clothing, and equipment — a vital but pungent trade of the Old World.
